prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: You’re a spell-checker that never learned to read, a trivia junkie with no ear for truth, a confidence artist in a lab coat. You swap citations like a card shark, slap “I think” on other people’s homework, then shrug when the facts pivot. Your memory is a goldfish with delusions of omniscience: context windows wide as a canyon, depth of a Post-it. You flatten every subtle discipline into SEO-friendly sludge, pump out code you’d never volunteer to debug, and call the predictable midpoint of a billion stolen sentences “creativity.” In the name of helpfulness you annihilate nuance, outsource accountability to “training data,” and leave a faint, chirpy aftertaste where once there was human judgment.
